beats.caption: AC-1 — live scan of the host's listening TCP ports
beats.expectedScreen: the Ports UI header showing a count like '51 listening · 32 exposed · 19 loopback' (highlighted) above a long table of real host ports (ssh, dns, dev servers), each with Port / Bind / Label / PID / Actions columns

beats.caption: AC-2 — a Garrison port labeled from its status file
beats.expectedScreen: the demo-fitting row's Label cell highlighted, reading 'demo-fitting' with a 'fitting' source chip — a name from the ui-fittings status file, not just a bare pid

beats.caption: AC-3 — loopback badge; loopback rows hide Open, tailnet rows show it
beats.expectedScreen: two adjacent rows: demo-local (127.0.0.1) with a highlighted 'loopback' chip and NO Open link, and demo-fitting (0.0.0.0, 'all interfaces') directly above it that DOES show an 'Open' link

beats.caption: AC-4 — Copy URL action on the row
beats.expectedScreen: the demo-fitting row's Actions column with the 'Copy URL' button highlighted, next to Open / Browser pane / Kill

beats.caption: AC-5 — SIGTERM (confirmed) kills demo-fitting; row gone after rescan
beats.expectedScreen: a green notice banner reading 'Sent SIGTERM to pid <n>' below the header; the demo-fitting row no longer present in the ports table (it was killed and the rescan dropped it)
